visa_types.name: Short Term (Visas Upto 90 Days)
visa_types.visa_fees:
The Visa application fee is 80 euros. More information on the visa fees can be found at the UDI web page:
You will pay the visa fee when you register your application on the Application Portal Norway.
VFS Service Charges
In addition to the Visa Fees, applicant requires to pay the VFS Service Charge of (33 Euros) which is nonrefundable.
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added services. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the service fee when you avail these value added services. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Lodging an application/when to apply?
It is advisable to lodge the application well in advance and no later than 15 days before the intended visit. Please note that applications can be lodged no more than six months before the start of the intended visit (nine months for seafarers).
Competent member state
If you plan to visit more than one Schengen country in the course of one trip, or if you plan several separate trips within a period of two months, you must submit your application to the embassy or consulate of the Member State that is your main destination based on the length of your stay, or the purpose of stay.
If no main destination can be determined, the Member State whose external border the applicant intends to cross first in order to enter the territory of the Member States.
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time (including sending the application back and forth to the Embassy in Amman) is 25 days.
Most cases are processed by the embassy, but if the embassy is in doubt about whether you can be granted a visa, they will forward your case to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration in Oslo (UDI). In such cases, applicants will receive an answer within 45 days after the application is received and registered by the embassy.
Note that it will take a couple of days from when the case is delivered at the application center, consulate or embassy until it is registered at the embassy. The waiting time starts from the day the application is registered at the embassy.
For more information, please check: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Please go to the home page of the Directorate of Immigration in Norway (UDI (https://www.udi.no/en)) for Information on application procedures and regulations for short term visits to Norway/Schengen.
Checklists:
Israel Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4ahlAOEKh97R0Hw3mkAYxp/04c2b10a97827d2805d99a96cc26de56/israel-checklist-2022.pdf)
Israel EU Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2pLxPeBwTqqc6SN6h61br7/6a9dfcfedb548e587f3cef20ef54df86/checklist-visa-eu-eea-family-members.pdf)
Applicant who are applying for Tourist Visa and will be staying in any paid accommodation while in Norway are requested to download the Travel Questionnaire fill it and bring it along with the other documents to apply for the visa click here
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Please go to the home page of the Directorate of Immigration in Norway (UDI (https://www.udi.no/en)) for Information on application procedures and regulations for short term visits to Norway/Schengen.
Checklists:
Israel Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4ahlAOEKh97R0Hw3mkAYxp/04c2b10a97827d2805d99a96cc26de56/israel-checklist-2022.pdf)
Israel EU Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2pLxPeBwTqqc6SN6h61br7/6a9dfcfedb548e587f3cef20ef54df86/checklist-visa-eu-eea-family-members.pdf)
Applicant who are applying for Tourist Visa and will be staying in any paid accommodation while in Norway are requested to download the Travel Questionnaire fill it and bring it along with the other documents to apply for the visa click here

## Visa Fees
The Visa application fee is 80 euros. More information on the visa fees can be found at the UDI web page:
You will pay the visa fee when you register your application on the Application Portal Norway.
VFS Service Charges
In addition to the Visa Fees, applicant requires to pay the VFS Service Charge of (33 Euros) which is nonrefundable.
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added services. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the service fee when you avail these value added services. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Lodging an application/when to apply?
It is advisable to lodge the application well in advance and no later than 15 days before the intended visit. Please note that applications can be lodged no more than six months before the start of the intended visit (nine months for seafarers).
Competent member state
If you plan to visit more than one Schengen country in the course of one trip, or if you plan several separate trips within a period of two months, you must submit your application to the embassy or consulate of the Member State that is your main destination based on the length of your stay, or the purpose of stay.
If no main destination can be determined, the Member State whose external border the applicant intends to cross first in order to enter the territory of the Member States.

## Photo Specifications
Please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/) for the requirements for the photo that you must hand in with
                                                                        your application.

## Processing Time
The processing time (including sending the application back and forth to the Embassy in Amman) is 25 days.
Most cases are processed by the embassy, but if the embassy is in doubt about whether you can be granted a visa, they will forward your case to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration in Oslo (UDI). In such cases, applicants will receive an answer within 45 days after the application is received and registered by the embassy.
Note that it will take a couple of days from when the case is delivered at the application center, consulate or embassy until it is registered at the embassy. The waiting time starts from the day the application is registered at the embassy.
For more information, please check: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)

visa_types.name: Residence Permits
visa_types.visa_fees:
Kindly click here for Visa Application Fees
VFS Service Charges
In addition to the Visa Fees, applicant requires to pay the VFS Service Charge of (33 Euros) which is nonrefundable.
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added services. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the service fee when you avail these value added services. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Visa Stamping
Once the applicants receive an approval for residence permit from UDI, the applicant will have to visit the nearest VFS office to submit their passport.
Pre-payment of VFS service charges are not required for visa stamping. We encourage applicants to book an appointment by selecting VISA stamping option in the appointment module to submit their passports OR they can walk between Sunday to Thursday from 09:00 - 12:00 hrs.
Bio metrics in residence cases
Norway Residence Case Bio metric started from 18th March 2021 and applicant will come in person is mandatory.
The requirement of digital signature is only for applicants who applying for residence permit over the age of 10.
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time (including sending the application back and forth to the Embassy in Amman) is 25 days.
Most cases are processed by the embassy, but if the embassy is in doubt about whether you can be granted a visa, they will forward your case to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration in Oslo (UDI). In such cases, applicants will receive an answer within 45 days after the application is received and registered by the embassy.
Note that it will take a couple of days from when the case is delivered at the application center, consulate or embassy until it is registered at the embassy. The waiting time starts from the day the application is registered at the embassy.
For more information, please check: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This section is for applicants who are going to reside or stay in Norway for more than 90 days.
For information on routines, regulations, case processing times, fees etc, please visit the UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/) webpage: Checklist
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This section is for applicants who are going to reside or stay in Norway for more than 90 days.
For information on routines, regulations, case processing times, fees etc, please visit the UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/) webpage: Checklist

## Visa Fees
Kindly click here for Visa Application Fees
VFS Service Charges
In addition to the Visa Fees, applicant requires to pay the VFS Service Charge of (33 Euros) which is nonrefundable.
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added services. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the service fee when you avail these value added services. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Visa Stamping
Once the applicants receive an approval for residence permit from UDI, the applicant will have to visit the nearest VFS office to submit their passport.
Pre-payment of VFS service charges are not required for visa stamping. We encourage applicants to book an appointment by selecting VISA stamping option in the appointment module to submit their passports OR they can walk between Sunday to Thursday from 09:00 - 12:00 hrs.
Bio metrics in residence cases
Norway Residence Case Bio metric started from 18th March 2021 and applicant will come in person is mandatory.
The requirement of digital signature is only for applicants who applying for residence permit over the age of 10.

## Photo Specifications
Please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/) for the requirements for the photo that you must hand in with
                                                                        your application.

## Processing Time
The processing time (including sending the application back and forth to the Embassy in Amman) is 25 days.
Most cases are processed by the embassy, but if the embassy is in doubt about whether you can be granted a visa, they will forward your case to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration in Oslo (UDI). In such cases, applicants will receive an answer within 45 days after the application is received and registered by the embassy.
Note that it will take a couple of days from when the case is delivered at the application center, consulate or embassy until it is registered at the embassy. The waiting time starts from the day the application is registered at the embassy.
For more information, please check: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)
